# www.studenthousing.co.in

> Student Housing offers fully furnished student hostels in Mumbai near top colleges across Vile Parle, Juhu, and Andheri with secure and comfortable living

## Home

[Book Hostels in Mumbai for college students | Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in): Student Housing offers fully furnished student hostels in Mumbai near top colleges across Vile Parle, Juhu, and Andheri with secure and comfortable living

## Listing

[Boys & Girls Hostel in Andheri West | Arcadia Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/arcadia-by-student-housing): Looking for a hostel in Andheri West? Arcadia by Student Housing provides furnished rooms, daily meals, Wi-Fi and a comfortable student living experience
[Luxury Boys Hostel in Juhu | Avenue by Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/avenue-by-student-housing): Live luxuriously at Avenue in Juhu. AC rooms, gym, pool & study areas. Just 7 mins from NMIMS. Twin & triple-sharing rooms available.
[Atlantis by Student Housing | Girls Hostel in Juhu near NMIMS](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/atlantis-by-student-housing): Atlantis offers luxury girls hostel with jacuzzi, gym, AC rooms & study areas. Just 1 min from NMIMS. Twin, triple & 4-sharing options.
[Elita by Student Housing | Luxury Boys Hostel in Juhu, Mumbai](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/elita-by-student-housing-boys): Looking for a boys hostel in Juhu? Elita offers furnished twin and triple-sharing rooms with meals, housekeeping, gym, pool & biometric safety
[Shradha Suman | Secure Girls Hostel in Vile Parle, Mumbai](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/shradha-suman-by-student-housing): Girls hostel near NMIMS & DJ Sanghvi with AC rooms, Zumba, 24/7 security & full-service flats. Twin, triple & four-sharing rooms.
[Modern Girls Hostel in Vile Parle | Crescenzo Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/crescenzo-by-student-housing): Secure girls hostel in Vile Parle with AC flats, 24/7 security, helpline & Zumba. Fully equipped rooms near NMIMS.
[Premium Boys Hostel in Vile Parle | Anand Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/anand-by-student-housing): Anand by Student Housing offers AC rooms, kitchen, turf & lounges. Just 5 mins from NMIMS. Twin, triple & 4-sharing available.
[Bharat by Student Housing | Boys Hostel near DJ Sanghvi](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/bharat-by-student-housing): Stay 100m from NMIMS. Bharat offers AC rooms, turf, kitchen & lounges. Ideal for BBA students. Triple sharing in Vile Parle.
[Moti Mahal | Boys Hostel in Vile Parle Near NMIMS, Mumbai](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/moti-mahal-by-student-housing): Premium boys hostel just 1 min from NMIMS. Moti Mahal offers AC rooms, kitchen, turf & lounges. Twin, triple & 4-sharing available.
[Elita by Student Housing – Girls Hostel in Juhu, Mumbai](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/elita-by-student-housing-girls): Elita by Student Housing is a girls hostel in Juhu near NMIMS offering twin & triple-sharing rooms, daily meals, housekeeping, gym, pool & biometric safety
[Aston by Student Housing – Hostels in Cuffe Parade Mumbai](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/aston-by-student-housing): Stay at Aston by Student Housing, a premium boy's hostel in Cuffe Parade, South Mumbai. Spacious rooms, modern amenities, and a prime location
[Hostel in Vile Parle Mumbai | Ganga Niwas by Student Housing](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/listing/ganga-niwas-by-student-housing): Premium boys hostel near NMIMS with AC rooms, kitchen, turf & lounges. Twin, triple & four-sharing available at Ganga Niwas.

[Tips For Making Friends In Hostels and Building a Network](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/tips-for-making-friends-in-hostels-building-network): Worried about feeling isolated in a new city? Here are some tips for making friends in hostels for college students. Read now!
[How Hostel Life Shapes Personality and Character Growth](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/how-hostel-life-shapes-personality-and-character-growth): Certain experiences may seem challenging at first, but when we find the courage to pursue them, they often turn out to be the most needed and rewarding. One such experience is living a hostel life. Students who live in a hostel consider it a transformative experience that not only prepares them for their professional journey but also contributes significantly to their personal growth.
[Common Myths About Student Hostels That You Should Be Aware Of](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/common-myths-about-student-hostels): Student life is one of the most crucial phases in a person's life. The choices made in this stage have the potential to shape one's long-term career path. But sometimes, this can also be the time when you feel stuck due to certain challenges, such as location.
[Is Hostel Life Good for Students? A Comprehensive Guide](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/is-hostel-life-good-for-students): Hostel life brings out strong opinions. Some people say it’s the most fun you’ll ever have. Others tell you it’s loud, chaotic, and full of rules. You’re likely hearing both sides if you’re getting ready to move out for college.
[Guide to Handling Common Hostel Roommate Problems](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/hostel-roommate-problems-guide): Sharing a hostel room can be one of the most rewarding experiences of student life. You build memories, learn independence, and often meet people who shape your journey.
[Hostel vs PG or Apartment – What’s Better?](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/hostel-vs-pg-vs-apartment): Choosing where to stay during your college years is not a small decision. It affects how you study, how you rest, and how you grow. You are not just picking a room. You are choosing your space for learning, living, and everything in between.
[Hostel Life Is Better Than Just Living at Home: 13 Reasons Why](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/why-hostel-life-is-better-than-home): More students today are choosing to stay in hostels during their college years. Some make the shift to save time. Others do it to feel closer to their academic environment. A few move out because it’s no longer possible to stay at home. But many stay on because hostel life changes them.
[Top 10 Reasons to Choose Hostel Accommodation for Students](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/reasons-to-choose-hostel-accommodation-for-students): In today's education landscape, rising student accommodation costs are pushing students to seek smarter, more affordable living options.
[Fun Hostel Weekend Activities to Help Students Socialise Better](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/fun-hostel-weekend-activities-for-students): Have you ever wondered how some hostel students enjoy hostel life and others struggle with loneliness? Every weekend, thousands of hostel students scroll through social media, and they feel disconnected even when surrounded by potential friends and study partners.
[How Is Life in a Hostel? Balance Academics & Social Connection](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/hostel-life-balancing-academics-and-social-life): Starting life in a hostel can be an emotionally challenging and exciting phase which is filled with new people, independence, and limitless possibilities and opportunities.
[Benefits of Hostel Life on Student Success](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/benefits-of-hostel-life-on-student-success): For students starting their higher education journey, the choice of accommodation plays a crucial role.
[What Are The Do’s And Don’ts Of Living In A Hostel? Basic Etiquette](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/hostel-living-dos-and-donts): Have you ever woken up at 2 a.m. because of a roommate's noisy phone call? Or did you find the hall or room in a mess? Are you a student staying in a hostel or preparing to stay in one? Then, it is high time to understand that communicable living comes with a book of rules.
[Personal Hygiene Tips for Students in Shared Hostel Spaces](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/personal-hygiene-tips-hostel): Living in a hostel? It is not just a place to sleep; it is your second home, your study area, and your social hub. Hostel life makes people independent and offers a new set of people to bond with, but it also means shared spaces, limited privacy, and exposure to a variety of personal habits.
[First Week at a Hostel? Here’s What to Expect and How to Adjust](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/hostel-first-week-guide-adjustment-tips): The night before you move to your hostel is often a mix of excitement, nerves, and a long checklist all arriving at once. What will it be like? How do you prepare for a hostel stay as a student so you can focus on studies and friends, not logistics? This short guide on “first week at a hostel” answers those questions with practical, expert-backed steps, and insider pointers that make “what it’s like to live in a hostel for the first time” easier to predict. Let’s dive in!
[Student Hostel Evolution: From Basic Lodging to Modern Living](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/evolution-of-student-hostels-modern-living): Have you ever been in a small, stuffy room with four other roommates, where the people are talking loudly, the aroma of parathas drifts, and the bathroom line is testing your patience? Classic student hostel life, right? But honestly, is this what we deserve, even in 2025, when stepping into university life?

It is not only about sharing the rent.
[Frustrated with Homesickness in a Hostel? Here’s How to Cope](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/homesickness-in-hostel-how-to-cope): Ever stared at your hostel ceiling, wishing for your mom’s dal-chawal or an evening-long walk with your friends?
[From Hostel Room To Startup Hub: Student Entrepreneur Stories](https://www.studenthousing.co.in/blogs/student-entrepreneur-stories): Imagine it’s past midnight, the corridor lights are dim, someone’s finishing an assignment, someone else is rehearsing a pitch, and a small group is sketching product ideas on the back of a notebook.

The pressure to perform, the fear of falling behind, and never-ending study exhaustion can drain even the most prepared brains, and staying away from home adds another layer of challenge.
